# Koan Framework

> A .NET 10 meta-framework for agentic, data-driven web applications — "Rails for .NET"
> for the era where AI capability is part of the domain model. One grammar — `Entity<T>` —
> covers CRUD, REST, vector search, background jobs, caching, embeddings, and agent (MCP) tools.

> Status: pre-1.0, consolidation phase (version via NBGV, currently 0.17.x). The core —
> entity/data pillar, web, cache, jobs, vector — is settled and integration-tested; several
> outer pillars are experimental. Until 1.0, build from source — published packages
> (`Sylin.Koan.*` on NuGet) lag the repo.

## The whole framework in three beats

### 1 · Model an entity → get an application

The entire bootstrap. The web pipeline, controllers, health endpoints, and discovery are
auto-wired when `Koan.Web` is referenced — "Reference = Intent".

```csharp
var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);
builder.Services.AddKoan();
var app = builder.Build();
app.Run();
```

Model + API:

```csharp
using Koan.Data.Core.Model;
using Koan.Web.Controllers;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc;

public sealed class Todo : Entity<Todo>
{
    public string Title { get; set; } = "";
    public bool IsCompleted { get; set; }
}

[Route("api/[controller]")]
public sealed class TodoController : EntityController<Todo> { }
```

That's a full REST API: GET/POST/DELETE with pagination and querying, GUID v7 ids generated
on first read, `/api/health`, structured logging, and zero-config SQLite (`./data/app.db`).
Schema is created automatically in development.

Working with data (the verbs — note: `Query`, not `Where`; `Remove`, not `Delete`):

```csharp
var todo  = await Todo.Get(id);                      // null when missing
var open  = await Todo.Query(t => !t.IsCompleted);   // pushed down when the adapter supports it
var page  = await Todo.FirstPage(20);
await new Todo { Title = "Ship it" }.Save();
await todo.Remove();
await foreach (var t in Todo.AllStream(batchSize: 1000)) { /* streaming, constant memory */ }
```

### 2 · Reference an intent → gain a capability

Adding a package reference (plus, at most, one attribute) is the configuration. Same entities,
same controllers — Postgres storage, transparent `[Cacheable]` L1/L2, durable `IKoanJob<T>`
background work, `[Embedding]` semantic search, `[McpEntity]` agent tools. Backends differ and
Koan refuses to pretend otherwise: every adapter declares its capabilities, the framework
negotiates them, and an unsupported operation fails loudly instead of silently degrading.

### 3 · The app explains itself

The boot report printed at startup names every discovered module, the elected adapters, and the
boot phases — it is the primary debugging surface. Capability sets are queryable at runtime;
well-known endpoints describe the running service. Discovery is a Roslyn source-generated
registry compiled into your assemblies: deterministic, ordered, AOT-friendly — not runtime
reflection magic.

## The concept budget (8 concepts)

Hello-CRUD needs exactly eight Koan concepts; nothing else is required:

1. `Entity<T>` — your model, with a string `Id` (GUID v7, generated on first read)
2. Entity statics & instance verbs — `Get` / `All` / `Query` / `Save()` / `Remove()`
3. `EntityController<T>` — the REST surface over an entity
4. `AddKoan()` — the single bootstrap call ("Reference = Intent": referenced packages register themselves)
5. Provider election — the referenced data connector wins; zero-config defaults (SQLite → `./data/app.db`)
6. The boot report — what the app prints at startup is the primary debugging surface
7. Auto schema ("magic") — created in Development; gated by `Koan:AllowMagicInProduction` elsewhere
8. Web defaults — controllers, `/api/health`, secure headers auto-wired; JSON is camelCase, nulls omitted (Newtonsoft.Json)

## Anti-patterns (immediate red flags)

These trigger the `koan-entity-first` skill — do not write them:

- Manual `IRepository<T>` interfaces
- Injecting repositories into services
- Manual service registration in Program.cs (except via `KoanModule` / `KoanAutoRegistrar`)
- Custom ORM/DbContext usage instead of Entity<T>
- Provider-specific code without capability detection

## The canonical way

| Concern | Canonical way |
|---|---|
| Bootstrap | `KoanModule` (module primitive) + `AddKoan()` — never manual `Add*()` where a registrar exists |
| Serializer | Newtonsoft.Json, camelCase, nulls omitted |
| Scheduling / background work | `Koan.Jobs` (`IKoanJob<T>` + `JobContext`) |
| Realtime | SSE |

## Pointers

- `CLAUDE.md` — agent entry point: pattern → skill table, core principles, framework utilities
- `.claude/skills/` — on-demand framework knowledge (entity-first, bootstrap, api-building, …)
- `docs/getting-started/overview.md` — the golden path, concept by concept (source of the snippets above)
- `docs/assessment/00-overview.md` — the framework's own assessment: what is settled vs experimental
